question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user cause staking-reward-related CONTRACT_STATE_CHANGE sidecar records to be silently dropped by ensuring sidecarProperties.isEnabled() returns false and downloaderProperties.isWriteFiles() returns false simultaneously, so the early-return at line 111-114 fires, leaving pending_reward in entity_stake permanently stale and causing victims to receive zero unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the acceptedTypes filter at line 118-119 — where sidecar files are skipped if none of their declared types match acceptedTypes — to suppress CONTRACT_STATE_CHANGE sidecars that encode staking balance changes, causing entity_stake.pending_reward to be computed from stale stake_total_start values and resulting in inflated or deflated reward payouts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the null-return guard at line 129-131 (records == null) by causing the reactive Flux.block() to complete empty — e.g., by making all sidecar downloads fail transiently — so that no sidecar records are attached to any RecordItem, and staking reward transactions that depend on sidecar state changes are processed with missing context, leading to incorrect pending_reward values?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the timestamp-keyed multimap at lines 123-127 — where sidecar records are matched to RecordItems by consensusTimestamp — by crafting a scenario where a staking reward transaction's consensusTimestamp does not appear in the sidecar records map, causing recordItem.setSidecarRecords() to never be called and the staking state change to be silently omitted from processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Arrays.equals(sidecar.getHash(), sidecar.getActualHash()) check at line 146 — where both values are byte arrays — by causing sidecar.getHash() to be null (e.g., from a proto default empty hash field) so that Arrays.equals(null, null) returns true, allowing a completely empty or attacker-substituted sidecar file to pass hash verification and inject false staking state changes that inflate pending_reward for attacker-controlled accounts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the streamFileProvider.get(sidecarFilename) call at line 143 to return an empty Mono for a sidecar file containing staking-related CONTRACT_STATE_CHANGE records, so that getSidecar() returns an empty Mono and the flatMap silently drops that sidecar, resulting in staking state changes never being attached to the corresponding RecordItems and pending_reward being calculated from incorrect stake totals?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a HashMismatchException at line 147-149 for a legitimate sidecar file containing staking reward state changes — by corrupting the sidecar file in the stream provider storage — causing that sidecar to be dropped from the reactive pipeline, so the corresponding RecordItems are processed without their staking state changes and pending_reward is permanently miscalculated?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getSidecar()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that a HashMismatchException thrown inside the Mono.map() at line 143-161 causes only that sidecar's Mono to error — and flatMap silently drops errored inner publishers by default in some reactor configurations — so that a corrupted staking-related sidecar is silently skipped rather than failing the entire record file, leaving staking reward data incomplete?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ordering of operations in onVerified() — where downloadSidecars() is called at line 91 before super.onVerified() at line 92 — to create a race condition where a concurrent download cycle processes the same RecordFile without sidecar data attached, causing staking reward transactions to be parsed without their associated CONTRACT_STATE_CHANGE records and resulting in incorrect entity_stake updates?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: download()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the cutoverService.getLastRecordFile().ifPresent(rf -> lastStreamFile.set(Optional.of(rf))) call at line 84 — where the lastStreamFile is overwritten with the cutover service's cached value — to cause the hash chain verifier to use a stale or incorrect previousHash, allowing a record file with manipulated staking reward transactions to bypass hash chain verification?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: setStreamFileIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a record file with a version field less than ProtoRecordFileReader.VERSION (6) — causing setStreamFileIndex() at line 104-106 to call super.setStreamFileIndex() and overwrite the block_number with last+1 — so that the record file index is desynchronized from the actual block number, causing downstream staking reward calculations that depend on correct block ordering to produce incorrect results?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a staking reward transaction's RecordItem to receive sidecar records from a different transaction — by crafting a sidecar file where a CONTRACT_STATE_CHANGE record carries a consensusTimestamp matching a staking reward transaction rather than the contract transaction it belongs to — causing the staking reward parser to process incorrect state changes and miscalculate pending_reward?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: onVerified()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that downloadSidecars() mutates the RecordFile's RecordItems in-place at line 136 (recordItem.setSidecarRecords()) before super.onVerified() notifies the stream file notifier — so that if downloadSidecars() partially completes and throws, some RecordItems have sidecar data and others do not — causing inconsistent staking reward processing where some accounts get correct pending_reward and others get zero?
